decisions.title: Let Filesystem Isolation Follow Task Ownership
decisions.description: Worktree isolation is presented as a first-class runtime capability. A task can claim not just responsibility, but also an isolated filesystem view where its changes live.
decisions.alternatives: A single shared checkout is simpler, but it makes concurrent edits collide and weakens the boundary between independent workstreams.

zh.title: 让文件系统隔离跟着任务归属走
zh.description: worktree 隔离被提升为正式运行时能力。任务不仅能声明负责范围，还能拥有自己隔离的文件视图。

ja.title: ファイルシステム隔離をタスク所有権に追随させる
ja.description: worktree 分離を一級のランタイム機能として扱い、タスクごとに独立したファイル視点を持たせます。

decisions.title: Pair Isolation With An Event Log
decisions.description: Isolation is much easier to trust when each task also records lifecycle events. The event log turns separate workspaces into an inspectable system instead of hidden branches.
decisions.alternatives: Worktrees alone isolate files, but they do not explain what happened or why a workspace exists.

zh.title: 把隔离和事件日志配套起来
zh.description: 只有当每个任务同时记录生命周期事件时，隔离能力才真正可信。事件日志让这些独立工作区变成可检查系统。

ja.title: 隔離にはイベントログを組み合わせる
ja.description: 各タスクがライフサイクルイベントを記録してこそ、分離された作業空間を信頼しやすくなります。
